title: koeleria johnstonii subsp. mattheii
intro: Koeleria johnstonii subsp. mattheii, also known as Johnston's grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the mountainous regions of eastern Africa. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and dysentery. The health benefits of Koeleria johnstonii subsp. mattheii include its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
